jokes.text: It's okay, I guess. Why am I being modest? I'm alone. It's perfect.
jokes.setup: Alex evaluates something hesitantly
jokes.punchline: Self-aware realization he can drop false modesty when alone
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Alex's tendency toward self-deprecation and honesty. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: I bet you're wondering how you got a cab so fast in this rain. Well, it's because I've been sitting here admiring this window display. You see, I work here part-time at this art gallery.
jokes.setup: Elaine offers explanation for quick cab service
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's been distracted by her own work
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor showing Elaine's pride in her art work, though fairly mild.

jokes.text: You charmed me into it.
jokes.setup: Passenger rudely demands service
jokes.punchline: Elaine sarcastically claims to be 'charmed' by his rudeness
jokes.explanation: Clean sarcastic response, economical delivery but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Thanks, but I've seen Jersey.
jokes.setup: Elaine offers to start over from where she picked him up
jokes.punchline: Passenger dismisses New Jersey with casual disdain
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure - perfect dismissive New Yorker attitude in three words.

jokes.text: Rape... rape... rape!
jokes.setup: Passenger refuses to leave cab despite Elaine's demand
jokes.punchline: Elaine desperately screams 'rape' as her solution
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor that's shocking but fits the character's desperation.

jokes.text: You may have a little trouble getting that story to stick when the police find out I'm the National Secretary for the Gay Liberation Force.
jokes.setup: Elaine screams rape
jokes.punchline: Passenger reveals information that would make rape accusation implausible
jokes.explanation: Smart misdirection with good structure - completely deflates Elaine's threat with unexpected information.

jokes.text: He's faster than he looks.
jokes.setup: Passenger runs away quickly to avoid paying full fare
jokes.punchline: Elaine's underwhelmed observation about his speed
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to an unexpected situation - economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Did I tip my hand again? Only to the trained eye.
jokes.setup: Tony asks if he revealed his poker hand
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response suggests it was incredibly obvious
jokes.explanation: Standard poker humor with decent sarcasm but nothing exceptional.

jokes.text: Hey, Bobby, she thinks she had a bad fare. What happened? I had the QANTAS koala bear get carsick in my cab. And you don't want to know what a koala bear does when he gets carsick.
jokes.setup: Discussion of bad fares
jokes.punchline: Bobby's absurd story about a koala bear passenger with graphic implications
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd escalation - the specificity of 'QANTAS koala bear' makes it funnier, plus the gross-out implication.

jokes.text: Yeah. You mean the place where you work as a receptionist.
jokes.setup: Elaine complains about being treated like a receptionist at the gallery
jokes.punchline: Alex points out she IS a receptionist
jokes.explanation: Alex's characteristic dry honesty deflating Elaine's complaint. Well-timed and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: No wonder you're down. You realized your lifelong ambition.
jokes.setup: Elaine says she feels strange after success
jokes.punchline: Alex sarcastically suggests success is the problem
jokes.explanation: Sharp misdirection with perfect Alex delivery - economical and perceptive about human nature.

jokes.text: How can I turn down an invitation like that?
jokes.setup: Elaine says she wants someone there she doesn't care about impressing
jokes.punchline: Alex sarcastically accepts the backhanded invitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect response to an unintentionally insulting invitation - Alex's dry wit at its best.

jokes.text: I don't lie. I fudge.
jokes.setup: Alex accuses Elaine of lying about her job
jokes.punchline: Elaine's euphemistic distinction between lying and 'fudging'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Elaine's self-justification is perfectly economical and reveals her rationalization process.

jokes.text: The liar's punishment is not that he is not believed but that he cannot believe anyone else. John, George Bernard Shaw said that. He did? Your father was George Bernard Shaw?
jokes.setup: John quotes his father's wisdom about lying
jokes.punchline: Alex's literal interpretation that John's father was Shaw
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with solid structure - Alex's literal-mindedness creates the humor.

jokes.text: 'Cause we're nobodies. We're stupid, ignorant, low-life cabbies.
jokes.setup: Discussion of why they can't attend Elaine's party
jokes.punchline: Tony's blunt self-assessment of their social status
jokes.explanation: Tony's characteristic bluntness about class differences - uncomfortably honest and economical.

jokes.text: John, George Bernard Shaw said that. He did? Your father was George Bernard Shaw?
jokes.setup: Tony attributing quote to his father
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it's actually from famous playwright, leading to absurd conclusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that reveals Tony's character while creating absurd logic

jokes.text: Latka, in English. If they don't come, I don't come.
jokes.setup: Latka speaks in his native language
jokes.punchline: When asked to speak English, delivers perfectly clear ultimatum
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka moment - his loyalty to friends transcends language barriers, delivered with characteristic economy.

jokes.text: Perhaps some of those art-world cuties might want me to pose for them au naturel.
jokes.setup: Louie expresses interest in attending Elaine's art party
jokes.punchline: His delusional fantasy about nude modeling
jokes.explanation: Pure Louie - his complete lack of self-awareness about his physical appeal is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Phew! Who drive a sick koala bear?
jokes.setup: Latka encounters Bobby's cab
jokes.punchline: References Bobby's earlier koala bear story
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that extends Bobby's absurd story - Latka's broken English adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I can only imagine.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks about embarrassment of arriving first to a party
jokes.punchline: Alex's dry response suggests he's never been invited to such parties
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alex deadpan - economical and reveals class difference without being bitter.

jokes.text: Well, you can start anytime.
jokes.setup: Elaine wants Alex to comment on her apartment
jokes.punchline: Alex's silence forces her to prompt him explicitly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Elaine's insecurity and need for validation.

jokes.text: Ever heard of Betty's vodka? Gentlemen never tell.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks about off-brand liquor
jokes.punchline: Alex treats it like a sexual conquest with gentlemanly discretion
jokes.explanation: Clever application of gentlemanly code to cheap liquor - good wordplay with solid structure.

jokes.text: Gentlemen never tell.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking about Betty's vodka
jokes.punchline: Alex treating it like a sexual conquest
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning cheap vodka into romantic discretion

jokes.text: 12 Cans. Canned champagne? I guess for those macho gourmets who like to crush something after a sip.
jokes.setup: Elaine reveals she bought canned champagne
jokes.punchline: Alex's creative explanation for why champagne would come in cans
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor - the image of crushing a champagne can is both absurd and perfectly logical.

jokes.text: There's always the exception to the rule.
jokes.setup: Alex says guests are 'just people' as doorbell rings
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction when he sees who's at the door
jokes.explanation: Good timing joke - Alex's reassurance immediately proven wrong by whatever he sees.

jokes.text: Party overalls... in truck...
jokes.setup: Latka needs to change for the party
jokes.punchline: His 'party clothes' are apparently fancier overalls stored in his truck
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka character comedy - his idea of formal wear is completely logical within his worldview.

jokes.text: Yeah, I can see that.
jokes.setup: Elaine claims she's calmed down a lot
jokes.punchline: Alex's sarcastic response to her obvious continued anxiety
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response but well-timed and economical.

jokes.text: I am... chicken. I don't lie for the simple reason I am afraid of getting caught.
jokes.setup: Elaine accuses Alex of being super-ethical
jokes.punchline: Alex's honest self-assessment of cowardice rather than ethics
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Alex's self-awareness and honesty about his motivations is both funny and revealing.

jokes.text: What are you drinking? Aren't there a lot of phonies here? Let's go to my place. Was it good for you?
jokes.setup: Latka has learned party conversation
jokes.punchline: His collection includes wildly inappropriate pickup lines
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Latka's misunderstanding of social cues and conversation progression.

jokes.text: You've been teaching him. Not me. I was going to ask for a copy myself.
jokes.setup: Alex asks who taught Latka pickup lines
jokes.punchline: Tony denies it but wants the material for himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Tony - his admission reveals his own dating struggles.

jokes.text: Stop envying them.
jokes.setup: The other drivers leave the party early
jokes.punchline: Tony's blunt assessment of their departure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tony line - economical, blunt, and reveals his awareness of the class tension.

jokes.text: Aren't there a lot of phonies here? Let's go to my place.
jokes.setup: Latka tries his learned party lines on someone
jokes.punchline: Uses his inappropriate pickup material in sequence
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier pickup lines - less impact than first time but maintains character consistency.

jokes.text: It must be the way he says it.
jokes.setup: Latka's terrible pickup lines somehow work
jokes.punchline: Alex's bewildered attempt to explain Latka's success
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the mystery of attraction - Alex's confusion is relatable.

jokes.text: I put out oil fires. When an oil well catches on fire they call me in to put it out.
jokes.setup: Alex is pressed about his job
jokes.punchline: Creates elaborate lie about being an oil fire fighter
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Alex's reluctant lying - the specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: All holocausts may look alike but each one has a personality all its own.
jokes.setup: Alex elaborates on his oil fire fighting career
jokes.punchline: Treats oil fires like they have individual personalities
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - treating disasters as having personalities is brilliantly specific and unexpected.

jokes.text: I sort of like fudging.
jokes.setup: Alex admits he enjoyed lying
jokes.punchline: Uses Elaine's earlier euphemism for lying
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Alex's character growth - adopting Elaine's rationalization for his own behavior.

jokes.text: Well, actually, I had 12 wells go up at one time.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about his biggest fire
jokes.punchline: Alex escalates to an impossibly large disaster
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the oil fire lie - the specific number makes it more absurd.

jokes.text: Aren't there a lot of phonies here?
jokes.setup: Rita responds to Alex
jokes.punchline: Uses Latka's line, suggesting the party really is full of phonies
jokes.explanation: Callback with ironic twist - the line actually applies to the situation since Alex is lying.

jokes.text: Well, I'll forgive you if you'll forgive the cheer I'm about to deafen you with.
jokes.setup: Rita says she finds Alex attractive
jokes.punchline: Alex's over-the-top response showing his excitement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex's genuine excitement beneath his usual cool exterior.

jokes.text: Thanks. So you don't remember me, huh?!
jokes.setup: Elaine has been building up to confront the passenger
jokes.punchline: She finally explodes at the party
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Elaine's inability to let go destroys her party, which is both funny and painful.

jokes.text: Sure, I do. I just met you 30 seconds ago.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking if James remembers her
jokes.punchline: James's literal interpretation of when they met
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on literal vs intended meaning

jokes.text: What do you do for a living? Excuse me.
jokes.setup: James discovers Alex is also a cab driver
jokes.punchline: Immediately leaves the conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect class-conscious exit - economical and reveals the passenger's snobbery.

jokes.text: A man waits all his life for a woman like that. What's so great about her? She's the kind of woman only firefighters get.
jokes.setup: Alex mourns losing Rita
jokes.punchline: Maintains his oil fire fighter identity even in disappointment
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - Alex is so committed to his lie that he maintains it even when explaining his loss.

jokes.text: She called me garbage.
jokes.setup: Alex continues explaining how badly things went with Rita
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery of being called garbage
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of devastating personal insult - the understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You know how women feel about garbage. Always wondering if what you've thrown out was important so you start digging through it until you find it again.
jokes.setup: Alex says Rita called him garbage
jokes.punchline: Elaine's elaborate metaphor comparing men to garbage women reconsider
jokes.explanation: Creative extended metaphor with good observational truth about second-guessing decisions.

jokes.text: She spit on my shoes.
jokes.setup: Alex continues detailing his humiliation
jokes.punchline: Even worse insult than being called garbage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of humiliation - economical and adds physical insult to verbal.

jokes.text: I'll die if you don't forgive me.
jokes.setup: Elaine desperately seeks Alex's forgiveness
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overstatement of consequences
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic overstatement but fits Elaine's character and emotional state.

jokes.text: I don't know with those wild stories you're telling, you know?
jokes.setup: Alex forgives Elaine
jokes.punchline: References the absurd oil fire stories he told
jokes.explanation: Nice self-referential callback acknowledging the absurdity of his earlier lies.
